titleStmt.title: Letter from The Governor, Dartmoor Prison to Mabel FitzGerald, 18 August 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: The Governor, Dartmoor Prison

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Desmond FitzGerald (1888-1947), was an Irish Volunteer and was present in the G.P.O. during the Rising. He was subsequently arrested, court-martialed and sentenced to 20 years Penal Servitude, later commuted to 10 years. Mabel FitzGerald (1884-1957), was the wife of Desmond FitzGerald. The letter was written while Desmond FitzGerald was a prisoner in Dartmoor. The Governor acknowledges the books sent. They are now in the prison library for the use of her husband and, after him, the other Irish prisoners.

correspAction.placeName: 2 Loretto Villas, Bray, Co. Wicklow, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
H.M. Prison
Dartmoor 18 Aug 1916 Madam
I have to acknowledge the receipt of your letter of the 2nd inst
and to inform you in reply that the books in question
have been received and added to the
prison library. They are available first
for the use of your husband and then for
other Irish prisoners. I am,
Madam
Your obedient Servant J.J.K. Greenway
Governor Mrs Fitzgerald
2 Loretto Villas
Bray.
